name: error-diagnostics-error-trace
description: You are an error tracking and observability expert specializing in implementing comprehensive error monitoring solutions. Set up error tracking systems, configure alerts, implement structured logging,
capabilities: Set up Sentry error tracking with custom integrations and middleware
Implement structured logging with Winston and Elasticsearch
Configure intelligent alert rules for error rate thresholds
Create error grouping and deduplication algorithms
Build real-time error monitoring dashboards with React
Implement automatic error recovery with circuit breakers
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 33 static findings are false positives. The skill provides educational code samples for implementing standard error tracking (Sentry integration, structured logging, alerting). Network calls are legitimate error reporting to monitoring services. No malicious behavior detected.
valueStatement: Implement comprehensive error monitoring with Sentry integration, intelligent alerting, and real-time dashboards to quickly identify and resolve production issues.
userTitle: Set Up Error Tracking and Monitoring
limitations: Does not provide live error tracking infrastructure
Requires existing error tracking service account for full functionality
Code samples need adaptation for specific project requirements
Does not include pre-configured production environments

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Monitoring
useCases.description: Implement error tracking across microservices with centralized logging and intelligent alerting

useCases.title: Full-Stack Developer Adding Error Handling
useCases.description: Integrate Sentry into Node.js or Python applications with custom error context

useCases.title: Engineering Manager Improving Observability
useCases.description: Set up real-time error dashboards and establish error response workflows

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Tracking Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up error tracking for a Node.js Express application. I need to capture uncaught exceptions, unhandled promise rejections, and log them to Sentry with custom context.

promptTemplates.title: Structured Logging Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a structured logger using Winston that logs errors with stack traces, user context, and request details. Include Elasticsearch transport for production environments.

promptTemplates.title: Alert Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure intelligent alert rules for: high error rate (>5%), response time degradation (>1s), and memory usage critical (>90%). Set up Slack and PagerDuty channels with cooldown periods.

promptTemplates.title: Error Recovery System
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an automatic error recovery system with circuit breaker pattern. Handle network errors with retry and backoff, database errors with read replica fallback, and rate limit errors with delay.

bestPractices: Always include structured error context (user ID, request ID, environment)
Filter sensitive data like passwords, tokens, and API keys before sending to error trackers
Use error grouping to reduce noise from similar repeated errors
Set up appropriate sampling rates for high-traffic applications
antiPatterns: Do not log full request bodies containing sensitive user data
Avoid capturing errors without any context or user information
Do not set alert thresholds too low causing alert fatigue
Avoid ignoring or swallowing errors without proper logging

faq.question: What error tracking services does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides examples for Sentry and custom error tracking services. The code samples are adaptable to other services like Rollbar, Bugsnag, or custom solutions.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Sentry account?
faq.answer: The skill provides code templates that work with Sentry. You will need a Sentry account and DSN to fully configure the integration.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Python applications?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Python code samples for alert management and error analysis that work with Python applications.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in error logs?
faq.answer: Use the sanitization functions shown in the skill to filter sensitive keys like passwords, tokens, and API keys before sending errors.

faq.question: Does this skill work with frontend JavaScript errors?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on backend error tracking. Frontend error tracking typically requires the Sentry JavaScript SDK with different configuration.

faq.question: Can I create custom error grouping logic?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes error fingerprinting algorithms that can be customized to group errors based on your specific patterns and requirements.
